close_reason: Style: .into() on string literals is idiomatic for Box<dyn Error>
description:
src/lib.rs has several 'literal_string'.into() conversions to String for error messages (e.g. line 100, 107, 322, 323). 'into()' here goes through Into<String> for &str which is one of two equivalent paths to to_owned()/to_string(). All three are idiomatic in Rust 2024.

Stylistic note only: the codebase uses .into() consistently — keep it that way. Filed as opinion.
title: Error message strings use .into() instead of .to_string() or to_owned()
